notes: mistral-vibe mid-turn queue. Vibe's TUI queue (message_queue.py) holds a mid-turn follow-up IN MEMORY: enqueue_prompt writes nothing to disk, and start_drain_if_needed returns early while agent_running(), so the queued message reaches messages.jsonl ONLY when the drain dequeues it and starts a real turn — after turn 1's turn_done. Turn 1's working span is therefore contiguous (no synthetic user-line, no →ready flicker when the follow-up is typed), and the parser's ClearToolNames fires only at the legitimate queued-turn boundary. Unlike claudecode, vibe never folds the queued text into the current turn — the drain is gated on agent-not-running, so it always runs as a distinct subsequent turn: hence turn1_settle (ready) → queued_turn_start (working) → queued_turn_end (ready) are modeled explicitly. Single-birth adapter (session_<ts>_<hash> dir via SessionIDFromPath); session_birth anchored UNPINNED to start so a transient proc-<PID> presession or the real session (whichever the scanner mints first) satisfies the birth. Observations: agent=mistral-vibe; model=mistral-medium-3.5 (confirmed from live meta.json config.active_model, passes NormalizeModelName unchanged); tokens_nonzero=true (sidecar stats.context_tokens on turn_done); cost_nonzero=false (the vibe adapter emits no per-turn cost). PROVISIONAL on live emission ordering — record settles the two-turn interleaving and re-anchors the birth if needed. Driver: wait_turn is a stubbed SEAM (gap:wait_turn); record ports SEAMs 1/2/3 before driving.

text: Vibe session appears in ready at launch (pre-session proc-<PID> or the session_<ts>_<hash> id, whichever arrives first; single-birth, anchored UNPINNED to start)
First prompt (slow count-to-10) drives ready → working; turn 1 begins

A plain-text follow-up is typed into the TUI mid-turn-1 and held in vibe's in-memory queue (never written to messages.jsonl until dequeued). State must stay working through the rest of turn 1 — no synthetic user-line flicker to ready, no flicker to waiting (spec bullets 1 and 2)
Turn 1 completes (terminal text-only assistant → turn_done) → ready. The queued follow-up has NOT yet landed in the transcript

The drain engine (agent no longer running) dequeues the queued message and starts a NEW turn — this is when the queued user line lands in messages.jsonl, driving ready → working at a legitimate turn boundary (not a mid-turn flicker)
The queued turn completes → final state ready (spec bullet 3: the agent consumes the queued message and the cycle still ends at ready)
